briefdescription.para: For Done Handler.
For Error Handler.
For Done Handler.
For Error Handler.
For Error Handler.
For Done Handler.
For Error Handler.
For Error Handler.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XMcdma_ErrorHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 Chan_id, u32 ErrorMask)
memberdef.name: XMcdma_ErrorHandler

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XMcdma_TxErrorHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 Chan_id, u32 ErrorMask)
memberdef.name: XMcdma_TxErrorHandler

briefdescription.para: This function initializes an MCDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using an MCDMA core. Initialization of an MCDMA includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state and resets all the hardware configurations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XMcdma instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes an MCDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using an MCDMA core. Initialization of an MCDMA includes setting up the instance data and updating the driver filed with a proper values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XMcdma instance.

simplesect.para: When user calls this function he should ensure the hardware is in a quiescent state by resetting all the hardware Configurations.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the MCDMA core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance.

simplesect.para: This function resets all the configurations made previously.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the AWCACHE field of the SG interface With the user specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the AWCACHE value to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the ARCACHE field of the SG interface With the user specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the ARCACHE value to be written.

briefdescription.para: Check whether reset is done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA engine instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Start the DMA engine.

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel instance to be worked on

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if starting the hardware fails.

briefdescription.para: Stops the DMA engine.

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel instance to be worked on

briefdescription.para: Enables a particular channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel instance to be worked on

briefdescription.para: Gets the last serviced s2mm channel number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA engine instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Gets the last serviced mm2s channel number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA engine instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Dump the registers for a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Update Current Descriptor for the MCDMA Channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel instance to be worked on

briefdescription.para: Update Tail Desc for the Channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_DMA_ERROR if no valid BD available to put into current BD register

briefdescription.para: Using a memory segment allocated by the caller, This function creates and setup the BD Chain for the MCDMA Channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the address of the application memory region.
is the number of BDs to setup in the application memory region.

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM if Count is not positive.

briefdescription.para: This function Populates the BD Chain with the required buffer address and length fields.

detaileddescription.para: User can submit multiple buffers/buffer descriptors by calling this function multiple times

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the buffer address to which data should send/recv.
is the Amount of data user requested to send/recv.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the BdStartAddress or requested bdlen doesn't match for the driver requirements.

memberdef.argsstring: (XMcdma_ChanCtrl *ChanPtr, UINTPTR BufAddr, u32 Len, u32 *AppPtr, u16 Tuser, u16 Tid)
memberdef.name: XMcDma_Chan_Sideband_Submit

briefdescription.para: This function populates the BD Chain with the required buffer address, length APP(user application), TUSER and TID fields.

detaileddescription.para: User can submit multiple buffers/buffer descriptors by calling this function multiple times

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the buffer address to which data should send/recv.
is the Amount of data user requested to send/recv.
is the point to APP fields.
is the value presented on the beat that has TLAST.
is the value value presented on the beat that has TLAST.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the BdStartAddress or requested bdlen doesn't match for the driver requirements.

briefdescription.para: This function triggers/Starts the h/w by programming the Current and Tail Descriptors and enabling the particular channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..

briefdescription.para: Returns a set of BD(s) that have been processed by hardware.

detaileddescription.para: The returned BDs may be examined by the application to determine the outcome of the DMA transactions. Once the BDs have been examined, the application must call XMcdma_BdChainFree() in the same order which they were retrieved here.
If hardware has partially completed a packet spanning multiple BDs, then none of the BDs for that packet will be included in the results.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the maximum number of BDs to return in the set.
is an output parameter, it points to the first BD available for examination.

simplesect.para: The number of BDs processed by hardware. A value of 0 indicates that no data is available. No more than BdLimit BDs will be returned.
Treat BDs returned by this function as read-only.

briefdescription.para: Frees a set of BDs that had been previously retrieved with XMcdma_BdChainFromHW().

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the number of BDs to free.
is the head of a list of BDs returned by XMcdma_BdChainFromHW().

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the set of BDs was freed.

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XAxiMcDma function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to ensure mutual exclusion.

briefdescription.para: Set the Buffer descriptor buffer address field.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is the address to set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if buffer address set successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if hardware has no DRE and address is not aligned

briefdescription.para: Set the control bits for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the bit value to set

briefdescription.para: Set the APP word at the specified APP word offset for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is the offset inside the APP word, it is valid from 0 to 4
is the value to set

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM under following error conditions. 1) StsCntrlStrm is not built in hardware 2) Offset is not in valid range

briefdescription.para: Get the APP word at the specified APP word offset for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the offset inside the APP word, it is valid from 0 to 4
is to tell the caller whether parameters are valid

simplesect.para: The APP word. Passed in parameter Valid holds 0 for failure, and 1 for success.

briefdescription.para: Set interrupt coalescing parameters for a particular channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on..
is the Irq Threshold value valid ranges are 1 to 255
is the Irq Delay value valid ranges are 1 to 255

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if Interrupt Coalescing settings updated
XST_FAILURE if IrqCoalesce and IrqDelay parameters are out of range

briefdescription.para: Dump the fields of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function is the Per Channel interrupt handler for the MCDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from Status register, determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in the per channel control register, and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XMcdma_ChanSetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Channel instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: To generate interrupt required interrupts should be enabled.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType.

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XMCDMA_HANDLER_DONE Channel Done handler XMCDMA_HANDLER_ERROR Channel Error handler

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel instance to be worked on
specifies which callback is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the S2MM(RX) interrupt handler for the MCDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from Status register, determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in global control register, and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XMcdma_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: To generate interrupt required interrupts should be enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is the MM2S interrupt handler for the MCDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from Status register, determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in global control register, and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XMcdma_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: To generate interrupt required interrupts should be enabled.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType.

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XMCDMA_TX_HANDLER_DONE MM2S(TX) Done handler XMCDMA_TX_HANDLER_ERROR MM2S(TX) Error handler XMCDMA_HANDLER_DONE S2MM(RX) Done handler XMCDMA_HANDLER_ERROR S2MM(RX) Error handler XMCDMA_HANDLER_PKTDROP S2MM(RX) Packet drop handler

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMcdma instance to be worked on.
specifies which callback is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the MCDMA driver.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the header file xmcdma.h for more detailed information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XMcDma instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the test is successful.
XST_FAILURE if the test is failed.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Disable Particular Channel in the MCDMA Core.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.
is the channel to disable.

briefdescription.para: Get Channel number that last Serviced.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether DMA is Idle or not.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.

listitem.para: FALSE if MCDMA is not idle

memberdef.name: XMcdma_IntrEnable
memberdef.initializer: (XMcdma_WriteReg((Chan)->ChanBase, \ (Chan->Chan_id - 1) * XMCDMA_NXTCHAN_OFFSET + XMCDMA_CR_OFFSET, \ XMcdma_ReadReg((Chan)->ChanBase, (Chan->Chan_id - 1) * \ XMCDMA_NXTCHAN_OFFSET + XMCDMA_CR_OFFSET) | \ ((Mask) & XMCDMA_IRQ_ALL_MASK)))

briefdescription.para: This function enables interrupts specified by the Mask in specified direction, Interrupts that are not in the mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.
is the mask for the interrupts to be enabled

memberdef.name: XMcdma_IntrDisable
memberdef.initializer: (XMcdma_WriteReg((Chan)->ChanBase, \ (Chan->Chan_id - 1) * XMCDMA_NXTCHAN_OFFSET + XMCDMA_CR_OFFSET, \ XMcdma_ReadReg((Chan)->ChanBase, (Chan->Chan_id - 1) * \ XMCDMA_NXTCHAN_OFFSET + XMCDMA_CR_OFFSET) & \ ~((Mask) & XMCDMA_IRQ_ALL_MASK)))

briefdescription.para: This function disables interrupts specified by the Mask.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not in the mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.
is the mask for the interrupts to be disabled

briefdescription.para: Gets global Packet drop Count.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Gets the TX Channel Pointer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA engine instance to be worked on.
is the channel to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: XMcdma_ChanCtrl *XMcdma_McdmaTxChan(XMcdma * InstancePtr, u32 ChanId)

briefdescription.para: Gets the RX Channel Pointer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA engine instance to be worked on.
is the channel to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: XMcdma_ChanCtrl *XMcdma_McdmaRxChan(XMcdma * InstancePtr, u32 ChanId)

briefdescription.para: Gets the Channel BD Chain Current BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to Operate on.

simplesect.para: Channel BD Chain current Buffer descriptor.

briefdescription.para: This functions gives the Buffer descriptor count processed by the h/w.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to Operate on.

simplesect.para: Buffer descriptor count processed by the h/w.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the interrupts that are asserted.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to Operate on.

simplesect.para: The bit mask for the interrupts asserted.

briefdescription.para: This function acknowledges the interrupts that are specified in Mask.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to Operate on.
is the mask for the interrupts to be acknowledge

briefdescription.para: Get Packet drop Count for the particular Channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA Channel to be worked on.
is the Channel number to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the ARCACHE field with the user specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the ARCACHE value to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the AWCACHE field with the user specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the AWCACHE value to be written.

briefdescription.para: Get Egress Channel Observer contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on.
is the Egress Channel observer number to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Get Ingress Channel Observer contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on.
is the Ingress Channel observer number to be worked on.

memberdef.name: XMCDMA_CHAN_SEEKAHEAD
memberdef.initializer: { \ UINTPTR Addr = (UINTPTR)(void *)(BdPtr); \ \ Addr += (Chan->Separation * (NumBd)); \ if ((Addr > (Chan)->LastBdAddr) || ((UINTPTR)(BdPtr) > Addr)) \ { \ Addr -= (Chan)->Length; \ } \ \ (BdPtr) = (XMcdma_Bd *)(void *)Addr; \ }

briefdescription.para: Read the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to read
is the word offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XMcdma_BdRead(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Offset)

briefdescription.para: Read the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to read
is the word offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u64 XMcdma_BdRead64(u64 BaseAddress, u32 Offset)

briefdescription.para: Write the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to write
is the word offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the field

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XMcdma_BdWrite(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Write the given Buffer Descriptor double word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to write
is the word offset to be written
is the 64-bit value to write to the field

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XMcdma_BdWrite(u64 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u64 Data)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the status of a Ingress(S2MM) BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: Use XMDMA_BD_STS_*** values defined in xmdma_hw.h to interpret the returned value

briefdescription.para: Check whether a Ingress(S2MM) BD has completed in hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This BD has been submitted to hardware. The application can use this function To poll for the Completion of the BD.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on

listitem.para: XMCDMA_BD_STS_COMPLETE_MASK if completed, may contain XMDMA_BD_STS_*_ERR_MASK bits.

briefdescription.para: Get the actual transfer length of a Ingress (S2MM)BD.

detaileddescription.para: The BD has completed in hw.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on
is the Maximum Transfer Length.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: int XMcDma_BdGetActualLength(XMcdma_Bd* BdPtr, u32 LengthMask)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the status of a Egress(MM2S) BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: Use XMDMA_BD_STS_*** values defined in xmdma_hw.h to interpret the returned value

briefdescription.para: Check whether a Egress(MM2S) BD has completed in hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This BD has been submitted to hardware. The application can use this function To poll for the Completion of the BD.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on

listitem.para: XMCDMA_BD_STS_COMPLETE_MASK if completed, may contain XMDMA_BD_STS_*_ERR_MASK bits.

briefdescription.para: Get the actual transfer length of a Egress(MM2S) BD.

detaileddescription.para: The BD has completed in hw.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on
is the Maximum Transfer Length.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: int XMcDma_TxBdGetActualLength(XMcdma_Bd* BdPtr, u32 LengthMask)

briefdescription.para: Gets the control bits of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: Return the next BD in the Chain.

parameterdescription.para: is the Channel BD Chain to operate on.
is the current BD.

simplesect.para: The next BD in the Chain relative to the BdPtr parameter.
C-style signature: XMcdma_Bd XMcdma_BdChainNextBd(XMcdma_ChanCtrl Chan, XMcdma_Bd *BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Return the previous BD in the Chain.

parameterdescription.para: is the MCDMA channel to operate on.
is the current BD.

simplesect.para: The previous BD in the Chain relative to the BdPtr parameter.
C-style signature: XMcdma_Bd XMcdma_BdChainPrevBd(XMcdma_ChanCtrl Chan, XMcdma_Bd *BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DMA is started, meaning the channel is not halted.

parameterdescription.para: is the channel instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DMA channel is started, meaning the channel is not halted.

parameterdescription.para: is the channel instance to operate on.
is the channel number to operate on.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: int XMcdma_ChanHwIsStarted(XMcdma_ChanCtrl *Chan, u32 Chan_id)

briefdescription.para: Set the Tid and Tuser fields of a BD with the user provided values.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is Tid value to be written in the Bd field.
is Tuser value to be written in the Bd field.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XMcDma_BdSetCtrlSideBand(XMcdma_Bd* BdPtr, u8 Tid, u8 Tuser)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the side band status of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: Use XMDMA_BD_STS_*** values defined in xmdma_hw.h to interpret the returned value

briefdescription.para: Clears the BD Contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: Set the Sw ID field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: The ID is an arbitrary piece of data the application can associate with a specific BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 32 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XMcdma_BdSetSwId(XMcdma_Bd* BdPtr, void Id)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Sw ID field of the given BD previously set with XMcdma_BdSetSwId.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for MCDMA driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains static initialization methods for Xilinx MCDMA core.
This file contains the self-test function for the MCDMA core.
This file contains the implementation of the interrupt handling functions for MCDMA driver.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:
This file implements all the Scatter/Gather handling for the MCDMA Core, please see xmcdma.h for more details.
Refer to the header file xmcdma.h for more detailed information.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:
Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version. 1.1 rsp 20/02/18 Fix unused variable warning. Remove TimeOut variable.CR-979061 1.3 rsp 14/02/19 Populate HasRxLength value from config.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial Version. 1.2 mus 11/05/18 Support 64 bit DMA addresses for Microblaze-X platform. 1.3 rsp 02/11/19 Add top level submit XMcDma_Chan_Sideband_Submit() API to program BD control and sideband information. 1.4 rsp 09/17/19 Prefer using dmb in XMcdma_UpdateChanTDesc.
Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version. 1.2 mj 05/03/18 Added macro XMcdma_BdSetSwId and XMcdma_BdGetSwId to set and get Sw ID field from BD.
Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version. 1.2 mj 05/03/18 Defined XMCDMA_BD_SW_ID_OFFSET 1.2 rsp 08/17/18 Remove unused XMCDMA_BD_LEN_MASK
MODIFICATION HISTORY:
Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 adk 18/07/17 Initial version. 1.2 mj 05/03/18 Implemented XMcdma_LookupConfigBaseAddr() to lookup configuration based on base address.
